CREATE TABLE ip_ranges (id INT PRIMARY KEY, net INET);
SELECT inet '192.168.1.5' << net FROM ip_ranges WHERE id = 1;
CREATE TABLE user_ips (user_id INT, ip INET);
SELECT user_id FROM user_ips WHERE ip << (SELECT net FROM ip_ranges WHERE id = 1);
SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM user_ips) AS user_count, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ip_ranges) AS range_count;
SELECT user_id FROM user_ips WHERE ip << inet '192.168.1.0/24' OR ip << inet '10.0.0.0/8';
CREATE TABLE complex_conditions (condition_id INT, ip INET, net INET);
INSERT INTO complex_conditions VALUES (1, inet '192.168.1.10', inet '192.168.1.0/24');
SELECT condition_id FROM complex_conditions WHERE ip << net;
SELECT a.user_id FROM user_ips a INNER JOIN ip_ranges b ON a.ip << b.net WHERE b.id = 1;
